safe-delete-with-rm-try-it-1.title: Inspect the print room first
safe-delete-with-rm-try-it-1.prompt: The print room has a test page and an event plan. Type `ls` first so you can read both names before deciding what is temporary.
safe-delete-with-rm-try-it-1.hint: Use the terminal. Check your work with `pwd`, `ls`, `cat`, or `find` before clicking Run.

help.concept: This task checks the workspace you build, not one perfect command line.
help.hint_1: Create folders before putting files inside them.
help.hint_2: When you are unsure, inspect the folder first, then make the next small change.

check-before-rm.title: Check before deleting
check-before-rm.bodyMarkdown:
`rm` removes a file. In this beginner course, use it only for a clearly temporary file.

Before deleting, inspect the folder:

```bash
ls
```

Then remove the exact file you mean to remove.

rm-one-file.title: Remove one file with `rm`
rm-one-file.bodyMarkdown:
To remove one file, type the exact file name.

```bash
rm tmp-scratch.txt
```

Afterward, use `ls` to confirm it is gone. Do not use `rm -rf` in this course.

move-to-trash-alternative.title: When unsure, move instead of delete
move-to-trash-alternative.bodyMarkdown:
If you are not sure a file should be deleted, move it into a trash or archive folder instead.

```bash
mkdir -p trash
mv old-note.txt trash/old-note.txt
```

Moving is safer because someone can still recover the file later.
